commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=c3348ecc54d6e07449bc18c787cff89a4738428b)
commands.review_summary:
The branch changes the official external plugin catalog loader to use ClawHub feed/source profiles, validate hosted install candidate sourceRefs, resolve feed-style install metadata, and add catalog tests.

PR surface: Source +366, Tests +471. Total +837 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR defect: a live fetch of the new default ClawHub feed returned schemaVersion 2, while the PR head only accepts schemaVersion 1. I did not run the PR tests because this is a read-only review, but the source path makes the fallback behavior clear.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Protected maintainer-labeled draft PR with a live feed schema mismatch, missing contributor proof, and hosted marketplace trust decisions needs author and owner follow-up rather than an automated repair lane.

target.title: Add hosted catalog source profile validation
target.body:
<!-- hosted-feed-stack:start -->
## Full hosted marketplace feed stack

We are pausing this stack at PR8 for Vincent's review and takeover/shaping before wiring hosted marketplace data into startup, install, or automatic refresh behavior.

- PR1 [#95846](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95846): refactors the bundled external plugin catalog toward the feed-shaped data model.
- PR2 [#95868](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95868): adds the lazy hosted marketplace feed loader for the configured public feed URL, with guarded HTTPS fetch, checksum, schema, size, timeout, and bundled fallback behavior.
- PR3 [#95877](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95877): adds accepted hosted snapshot fallback so a failed or unchanged hosted fetch can reuse the last valid feed payload.
- PR4 [#95964](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95964): persists hosted marketplace feed snapshots in OpenClaw state.
- PR5 [#95969](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95969): adds marketplace source profile validation for hosted feed install candidates.
- PR6 [#95981](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95981): adds configurable `marketplaces.feeds` and `marketplaces.sources` profiles.
- PR7 [#96155](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96155): adds `openclaw plugins marketplace refresh` as the explicit manual snapshot refresh path.
- PR8 [#96158](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96158): adds `openclaw plugins marketplace entries` as the explicit manual read path over hosted, snapshot, or bundled fallback entries.

Next after review, not in this stack yet: decide whether PR9 should wire marketplace entries into install/search/startup, add automatic conditional refresh using `ETag` / `Last-Modified`, or move to ClawHub producer-side validation for `/v1/feeds/plugins` and `/v1/feeds/skills`.
<!-- hosted-feed-stack:end -->

## Stack

Step 5 in the hosted feed rollout. This is stacked on `feeds-hosted-catalog-persistent-snapshot` after:

- #95846 reshapes the bundled external plugin catalog as a feed-shaped fallback
- #95868 adds the guarded hosted feed loader
- #95877 adds the last-known-good snapshot fallback contract
- #95964 persists hosted catalog snapshots in OpenClaw state

## RFC alignment

This PR adds the first local feed/source profile scaffolding from RFC #19. The important boundary is that profile names are local OpenClaw configuration. A hosted feed can refer to a configured `sourceRef`, but it cannot define registry domains, credentials, or trust roots for the client.

This now matches the live ClawHub plugin feed at `https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins`, which uses `public-clawhub` candidates. The PR also keeps the older register host allowed for transition, while `registry.openclaw.ai` is still being brought online.

## Summary

- Adds local profile types for hosted catalog feeds and package sources
- Adds the default `clawhub-public` feed profile pointing at `https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins`
- Adds the default `public-clawhub` source profile for ClawHub candidates and keeps `public-npm` for npm-backed candidates
- Lets named local feed profiles authorize their configured HTTPS host through the existing SSRF guard path
- Keeps direct ad hoc `feedUrl` overrides constrained to the public host allowlist, including `clawhub.ai` and the earlier register host
- Filters feed entries that opt into `install.candidates[].sourceRef` when the source ref is missing or not configured locally
- Applies the same source-ref filtering to hosted responses and hosted snapshot fallback
- Recognizes RFC-style `id` and `title` fields for catalog identity and labels so live ClawHub entries are not dropped before the later install-mapping slice

## Deliberately not included

- Feed/source profile persistence in user config files
- Secret-reference resolution or authenticated feed/source access
- Signed feed envelopes or trust-key configuration
- Scheduled refresh, `maxStale`, or stale-catalog status reporting
- Changing plugin install execution to consume candidate source profiles or translating RFC candidates into installer specs
- Search, install UX, skills, or telemetry

## Validation

- `pnpm exec oxfmt src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint --deny-warnings src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `CI=1 timeout 180s node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`
